name: EnDa-WiFi-Exposer
summary: A simple tool for PenTesters (Ethical Hackers) for finding the passwords of saved networks after an exploitation

documentation.readme: A simple tool for PenTesters (Ethical Hackers) for finding the passwords of saved networks after an exploitation
usage_sections: Features: - Enumerating the passwords in a table - Fancy table - Some error catchings - An exe for post-exploitation

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with worknshubh/wifi-sukuna (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with tdiprima/wifi-is-radio (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with t6x/reaver-wps-fork-t6x (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['wifi_2_4_ghz', 'wifi_5_ghz', 'shell_linux'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category. Fits when the engagement needs: A simple tool for PenTesters (Ethical Hackers) for finding the passwords of saved networks after an exploitation.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A simple tool for PenTesters (Ethical Hackers) for finding the passwords of saved networks after an exploitation.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `python3 EnDaWiFiExposer.py`. Further invocations from upstream: `python EnDaWiFiExposer.py`. A simple tool for PenTesters (Ethical Hackers) for finding the passwords of saved networks after an exploitation.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Wireless, use EnDa-WiFi-Exposer because A simple tool for PenTesters (Ethical Hackers) for finding the passwords of saved networks after an exploitation.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 EnDaWiFiExposer.py` as documented upstream for EnDa-WiFi-Exposer.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python EnDaWiFiExposer.py` as documented upstream for EnDa-WiFi-Exposer.
When chaining Wireless tools, pair EnDa-WiFi-Exposer with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `EnDa-WiFi-Exposer --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
